title: Brain Protocol v2 agent card
description: The machine-readable agent card every system publishes at /.well-known/brain-protocol/card.json (BP-03 §2). The card describes the system, never a connection: nothing grant-specific, member-specific or secret appears in it. The published artefact is a JWS (RFC 7515) over the canonical card body, signed with a key listed in identity_keys, alg EdDSA (Ed25519); this schema validates the card body. Unknown card fields MUST be ignored (forward compatibility).

system_id.description: Stable, lower-case, globally unique by convention.

protocol_versions.description: Descending preference. Peers operate at the highest common version (BP-03 §3.1).

vocabulary.description: MUST for v2 - checked at connect (BP-03 §3.3, CD-6). A connection where either side's terms are neither base-vocabulary nor mapped does not proceed to sync.

mapping_table.description: URL of local-term mappings in the BP-01 §11 format.

tier.description: Per CD-2: Class D self-certifies with published TCK results; Classes A and H, and any S2 capability at any class, require verified certification.

last_pass.description: Date of the last green TCK run.

identity_keys.description: Long-lived card-signing keys (JWK, Ed25519). Distinct from per-grant keys (BP-03 §4). Rotation: new key published signed by the old one for a ≥ 30-day overlap (BP-03 §2.3.4).

not_after.description: Expiry date; an expired key aborts the connection attempt (BP-03 §2.3.2).

capabilities.description: What this system offers and consumes. write-direct never appears on a card because it never crosses a boundary (BP-03 §2.2, §5.2). An offer of an S2-ceiling capability at any class requires verified certification (CD-2).

direction.description: offer: we serve it; consume: we call yours.

sensitivity_ceiling.description: The highest class (BP-07) the capability ever carries. Never S3 - S3 never syncs.

audiences.description: OPTIONAL (v1.1, additive). The visibility tiers this capability may be shared at, in ascending order (e.g. private, shared:household). A consumer presents a per-capability audience picker over these tiers at connect and records the choice on the grant; an effective read clamps to the stricter of that choice and the grant ceiling (BP-03 §2.2). Absent: the consumer applies its own per-capability fallback, so a provider that omits it forgoes the picker.

auth.description: OPTIONAL token endpoint set. A provider that mints tokens through the connect handshake (BP-03 §6.4) rather than OAuth omits this entirely (as the reference provider does); when present, type is oauth2.1.

endpoints.description: At least one of a2a | mcp (BP-03 §2.2).

limits.description: Declared bounds (SHOULD; BP-02 §6, BP-04). Recommended defaults: 500 records or 1 MiB per batch, 60 requests/minute.
